question_stem: Marcus is a student in the third grade who shows marked difficulty with reading decoding and fluency. The building contains a reading specialist who happens to live in the same neighborhood. Without securing parental authorization, Marcus's teacher provided performance data from a standardized assessment to the specialist. Which statute has been violated through this casual information sharing?
Which pioneering theorist is best known for proposing that human brains contain innate biological structures that enable the acquisition of language and grammatical systems?

A superintendent plans to adopt an innovative mathematics curriculum that several parents find questionable. Seeking guidance, the district superintendent consults a school psychologist about recommended best practices for stakeholder involvement. Which recommendation should the psychologist prioritize?
A school psychologist is facilitating an RTI committee that is examining data collection methods. What are the two primary types of data that must be gathered throughout the RTI process?

A seventh-grade student in special education is exhibiting difficulty managing his behavior in the classroom setting. A school psychologist conducts a functional behavioral assessment which reveals the behavior is maintained by escape from difficult academic work. Based on these findings, what intervention would be most appropriate?
A school psychologist is explaining screening procedures to teachers at a professional development session. Universal screening measures are administered to all students at specified points to:

Parent and teacher adaptive behavior ratings showed considerable variation: the teacher's Vineland score was 62, the mother's Vineland showed 87, and the father's Vineland revealed 80. A 9th-grade instructor has a student with visual impairment who receives an involved assignment requiring a poster project. How should the educator best proceed?

A recently hired school psychologist starts at an under-resourced urban school. The principal provides a new cognitive battery for use, though the psychologist has no formal training with it. Parents have secured an educational advocate who formally requests that the new instrument be used because it has updated norms and shows cultural responsiveness. The tool is widely respected and would apply well. What should the school psychologist do?

domain_name: Services to Promote Safe and Supportive Schools

question_stem: Professional Learning Communities (PLCs) represent an evidence-based professional development framework centered on systematic examination of student outcome data. Elena is a junior at a mid-sized public high school. Early in the school year, Elena felt ostracized by Jessica. The new principal quickly stepped in and ended the bullying. Several weeks later, Elena informs administration that Jessica is once more making severe and threatening statements to her. The principal suspends Jessica for 2 days and contacts Jessica's parents. domain_name: Services to Promote Safe and Supportive Schools

Given the details of this case, the school psychologist can tell the school district which of the following?
A district-wide approach that receives broad administrative support and scientific evidence for effectiveness is most likely to be which of the following?

Case example: Andre is a ninth-grade student with above-average cognitive abilities. Despite his intelligence, he is currently earning mostly Cs in his classes. His teachers are suggesting that he may benefit from special education placement and additional support. A school psychologist is consulting with the team.

What should a school psychologist tell the teachers regarding special education placement for Andre?

Case example: Andre is a ninth-grade student with above-average cognitive abilities. Despite his intelligence, he is currently earning mostly Cs in his classes. His parents are very upset that he is making mostly Cs and are threatening to sue the school district. They are demanding that the school provide extensive special education services and accommodations.

Under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA), how should the school respond to the parents' threat of legal action?

The teacher randomly assigns 24 students to two groups: one group studies in complete silence, while the other group listens to classical background music played at moderate volume.
